Musical artist

**Bernard Lee** "**Pretty**" **Purdie** (born June 11, 1939) is an American [drummer](/wiki/Drummer "Drummer"), and an influential R&B, soul, [funk](/wiki/Funk "Funk") and jazz musician.[[1]](#cite_note-Purdie_in_Biog_Exc_of_Jazz-1) He is known for his precise time-keeping[[2]](#cite_note-Wynn-2) and his signature use of [triplets](/wiki/Tuplet "Tuplet") against a half-time backbeat: the Purdie [shuffle](/wiki/Shuffle_(music) "Shuffle (music)").[[3]](#cite_note-3) He was inducted into the *[Modern Drummer](/wiki/Modern_Drummer "Modern Drummer")* Hall of Fame in 2013.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

Purdie recorded *[Soul Drums](/wiki/Soul_Drums "Soul Drums")* (1968) as a band leader and although he went on to record *Alexander's Ragtime Band*, the album remained unreleased until *Soul Drums* was reissued on CD in 2009 with the *Alexander's Ragtime Band* sessions. Other solo albums include *[Purdie Good!](/wiki/Purdie_Good! "Purdie Good!")* (1971), *[Soul Is... Pretty Purdie](/wiki/Soul_Is..._Pretty_Purdie "Soul Is... Pretty Purdie")* (1972) and the [soundtrack](/wiki/Soundtrack "Soundtrack") for the [blaxploitation](/wiki/Blaxploitation "Blaxploitation") film *Lialeh* (1973).

In the mid-1990s, he was a member of [the 3B's](/wiki/The_3B%27s "The 3B's"), with [Bross Townsend](/wiki/Bross_Townsend "Bross Townsend") and [Bob Cunningham](/wiki/Bob_Cunningham_(musician) "Bob Cunningham (musician)").

## Biography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bernard_Purdie&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography")]

Purdie was born on June 11, 1939, in [Elkton, Maryland](/wiki/Elkton,_Maryland "Elkton, Maryland"), the 11th of 15 children.[[5]](#cite_note-5) At an early age, he began hitting cans with sticks and learned the elements of drumming techniques from overhearing lessons being given by Leonard Heywood. He later took lessons from Heywood and played in Heywood's big band. Purdie's other influences at that time were [Papa Jo Jones](/wiki/Jo_Jones "Jo Jones"), [Buddy Rich](/wiki/Buddy_Rich "Buddy Rich"), [Gene Krupa](/wiki/Gene_Krupa "Gene Krupa"), [Joe Marshall](/wiki/Joe_Marshall_(musician) "Joe Marshall (musician)"), [Art Blakey](/wiki/Art_Blakey "Art Blakey"),[[6]](#cite_note-Great_drummer-6) as well as [Cozy Cole](/wiki/Cozy_Cole "Cozy Cole"), [Sticks Evans](/wiki/Sticks_Evans "Sticks Evans"), [Panama Francis](/wiki/Panama_Francis "Panama Francis"), [Louis Bellson](/wiki/Louis_Bellson "Louis Bellson"), and [Herbie Lovelle](/wiki/Herbie_Lovelle "Herbie Lovelle").[[7]](#cite_note-7)

In 1961, he moved from Elkton to [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City"). There he played sessions with [Mickey and Sylvia](/wiki/Mickey_and_Sylvia "Mickey and Sylvia") and regularly visited the Turf Club on 50th and [Broadway](/wiki/Broadway_(Manhattan) "Broadway (Manhattan)"), where musicians, agents, and promoters met and touted for business. It was during this period that he played for the saxophonist [Buddy Lucas](/wiki/Buddy_Lucas_(musician) "Buddy Lucas (musician)"), who nicknamed him 'Mississippi Bigfoot'. Eventually Barney Richmond contracted him to play session work.[[6]](#cite_note-Great_drummer-6)

Purdie was contracted by arranger [Sammy Lowe](/wiki/Sammy_Lowe "Sammy Lowe") to play a session with [James Brown](/wiki/James_Brown "James Brown") in 1965 and recording session records also show that Purdie played on "Ain't That A Groove"[[8]](#cite_note-8) at the same session.[[6]](#cite_note-Great_drummer-6) Purdie is credited on James Brown's albums *[Say It Loud – I'm Black and I'm Proud](/wiki/Say_It_Loud_%E2%80%93_I%27m_Black_and_I%27m_Proud_(album) "Say It Loud – I'm Black and I'm Proud (album)")* (1969)[[9]](#cite_note-9) and *[Get on the Good Foot](/wiki/Get_on_the_Good_Foot_(album) "Get on the Good Foot (album)")* (1972).[[10]](#cite_note-10)

Purdie started working with [Aretha Franklin](/wiki/Aretha_Franklin "Aretha Franklin") as musical director in 1970 and held that position for five years,[[1]](#cite_note-Purdie_in_Biog_Exc_of_Jazz-1) as well as drumming for Franklin's opening act, saxophonist [King Curtis](/wiki/King_Curtis "King Curtis") and the King Pins.[[11]](#cite_note-11) From March 5 to March 7, 1971, he performed with both bands at the Fillmore West; the resulting live recordings were released as *[Aretha Live at Fillmore West](/wiki/Aretha_Live_at_Fillmore_West "Aretha Live at Fillmore West")* (1971) and King Curtis's *[Live at Fillmore West](/wiki/Live_at_Fillmore_West "Live at Fillmore West")* (1971).[[12]](#cite_note-Purdie_credits-12) His best known track with Franklin was ["Rock Steady"](/wiki/Rock_Steady_(Aretha_Franklin_song) "Rock Steady (Aretha Franklin song)"),[[13]](#cite_note-13) on which he played what he described as "a funky and low down beat". Of his time with Franklin he once commented that "backing her was like floating in seventh heaven".[[6]](#cite_note-Great_drummer-6)

Purdie was credited on the soundtrack album for the film *[Sgt. Pepper's Lonely Hearts Club Band](/wiki/Sgt._Pepper%27s_Lonely_Hearts_Club_Band_(soundtrack) "Sgt. Pepper's Lonely Hearts Club Band (soundtrack)")* (1978). He was the drummer for the 2009 [Broadway](/wiki/Broadway_(Manhattan) "Broadway (Manhattan)") revival of *[Hair](/wiki/Hair_(musical)#2009_Broadway_revival_and_2010_U.S._National_Tour "Hair (musical)")* and appeared on the associated Broadway cast recording.[[14]](#cite_note-14) In 2016, he was awarded an honorary doctorate in music by Five Towns College.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

Purdie has been a resident of [New Jersey](/wiki/New_Jersey "New Jersey"), living in [Edison](/wiki/Edison,_New_Jersey "Edison, New Jersey"), [Teaneck](/wiki/Teaneck,_New_Jersey "Teaneck, New Jersey") and [Springfield Township](/wiki/Springfield_Township,_Union_County,_New_Jersey "Springfield Township, Union County, New Jersey").[[16]](#cite_note-16) In 2023, he moved to [New Bern, North Carolina](/wiki/New_Bern,_North_Carolina "New Bern, North Carolina").

On 29 September 2024, Purdie was the recipient of the inaugural "Lifetime Achievement Award" at the UK Drum Show, held in [Liverpool](/wiki/Liverpool "Liverpool").[[17]](#cite_note-17)

On 13 September 2025, Purdie played at [Madison Square Garden](/wiki/Madison_Square_Garden "Madison Square Garden") with [Vulfpeck](/wiki/Vulfpeck "Vulfpeck"). Introduced by [Jack Stratton (musician)](/wiki/Jack_Stratton_(musician) "Jack Stratton (musician)") and helped to the stage by [Antwaun Stanley](/wiki/Antwaun_Stanley "Antwaun Stanley"), Purdie then played drums for the song *My First Car*, showcased the *Purdie Shuffle* to the crowd, and played drums for a cover of [Simply Beautiful](/wiki/Simply_Beautiful "Simply Beautiful").[[18]](#cite_note-18)

## Encounter Records

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bernard_Purdie&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Encounter Records")]

Purdie founded Encounter Records in 1973 and released five albums:

* EN 3000: [Seldon Powell](/wiki/Seldon_Powell "Seldon Powell") – *Messin' With Seldon Powell* (with [Jimmy Owens](/wiki/Jimmy_Owens_(musician) "Jimmy Owens (musician)"), [Garnett Brown](/wiki/Garnett_Brown "Garnett Brown"))
* EN 3001: Sands of Time – *Profile* (with Jimmy Owens, Garnett Brown, Seldon Powell)
* EN 3002: East Coast – *East Coast* (with [Larry Blackmon](/wiki/Larry_Blackmon "Larry Blackmon"), [Gwen Guthrie](/wiki/Gwen_Guthrie "Gwen Guthrie"), [Haras Fyre](/wiki/Haras_Fyre "Haras Fyre"))
* EN 3003: Frank Owens – *Brown 'N' Serve* (with [Hugh McCracken](/wiki/Hugh_McCracken "Hugh McCracken"))[[19]](#cite_note-19)
* EN 3004: [Harold Vick](/wiki/Harold_Vick "Harold Vick") as "Sir Edward" – *The Power of Feeling* (with [Victor Gaskin](/wiki/Victor_Gaskin "Victor Gaskin"))

## Drumming style

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bernard_Purdie&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Drumming style")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/2d/Purdie_shuffle.png/500px-Purdie_shuffle.png)](/wiki/File:Purdie_shuffle.png)

Purdie shuffle variants[[20]](#cite_note-Total-20) [Play](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/transcoded/c/cb/Purdie_shuffle.mid/Purdie_shuffle.mid.mp3 "Play audio")[ⓘ](/wiki/File:Purdie_shuffle.mid "File:Purdie shuffle.mid").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/ad/Purdie_shuffle_B.png/500px-Purdie_shuffle_B.png)](/wiki/File:Purdie_shuffle_B.png)

Purdie shuffle variant[[21]](#cite_note-Bible-21) [Play](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/transcoded/1/1a/Purdie_shuffle_B.mid/Purdie_shuffle_B.mid.mp3 "Play audio")[ⓘ](/wiki/File:Purdie_shuffle_B.mid "File:Purdie shuffle B.mid").

Purdie is known as a groove drummer with immaculate timing who makes use of precision [half note](/wiki/Half_note "Half note"), backbeats, and grooves.[[2]](#cite_note-Wynn-2) Purdie's signature sixteenth note hi-hat lick *pish-ship, pish-ship, pish-ship* is distinct.[[6]](#cite_note-Great_drummer-6) He often employs a straight eight groove sometimes fusing several influences such as swing, blues and funk. He created the now well-known [drum pattern](/wiki/Drum_pattern "Drum pattern") *Purdie Half-Time Shuffle* that is a [blues](/wiki/Blues "Blues") shuffle variation with the addition of syncopated [ghost notes](/wiki/Ghost_note "Ghost note") on the snare drum.[[21]](#cite_note-Bible-21) Variations on this [shuffle](/wiki/Shuffle_rhythm "Shuffle rhythm") can be heard on songs such as [Led Zeppelin](/wiki/Led_Zeppelin "Led Zeppelin")'s "[Fool in the Rain](/wiki/Fool_in_the_Rain "Fool in the Rain")", [the Police](/wiki/The_Police "The Police")'s "[Walking on the Moon](/wiki/Walking_on_the_Moon "Walking on the Moon")", and [Toto](/wiki/Toto_(band) "Toto (band)")'s "[Rosanna](/wiki/Rosanna_(song) "Rosanna (song)")" ([Rosanna shuffle](/wiki/Rosanna_shuffle "Rosanna shuffle")).[[21]](#cite_note-Bible-21)[[22]](#cite_note-NYT-22) Purdie plays the shuffle on [Steely Dan](/wiki/Steely_Dan "Steely Dan")'s "Babylon Sisters"[[23]](#cite_note-23) and "Home At Last".[[21]](#cite_note-Bible-21)

## Discography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bernard_Purdie&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Discography")]

### As leader/co-leader

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bernard_Purdie&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: As leader/co-leader")]

* *[Soul Drums](/wiki/Soul_Drums "Soul Drums")* (Columbia, 1967)

* *[Stand by Me (Whatcha See Is Whatcha Get)](/wiki/Stand_by_Me_(Whatcha_See_Is_Whatcha_Get) "Stand by Me (Whatcha See Is Whatcha Get)")* ([Mega Records](/wiki/Mega_Records "Mega Records") [in the 'Flying Dutchman Series'], 1972) with the Playboys

* *In the Pocket* (P-Vine, 1997)

* *The Masters of Groove Meet Dr. No* (Jazzateria, 2001) with [Reuben Wilson](/wiki/Reuben_Wilson "Reuben Wilson"), [Grant Green Jr.](/wiki/Grant_Green_Jr. "Grant Green Jr."), [Tarus Mateen](/wiki/Tarus_Mateen "Tarus Mateen")
* *King of the Beat* (3B's Music, 2001)
